business.headline: Why your salary is losing the race against home pricesIndia's housing dream is facing its biggest affordability test as rising prices force buyers to choose between bigger debts, longer commutes and delayed dreams.

FDs, SCSS or mutual funds? The best way to invest Rs 50 lakh after retirementRetiring with Rs 50 lakh may sound reassuring, but the real challenge begins after retirement: how do you turn that lump sum into a steady monthly income without running out of money?

US Justice Department okays Paramount's $110 billion Warner Bros takeoverAfter an eight-month review, the DOJ concluded the proposed merger would strengthen competition across streaming, television and film, potentially benefiting consumers, workers and the broader entertainment industry.

SpaceX opens at $150 in strong Nasdaq debut, up 11% from IPO priceSpaceX surged more than 20 per cent in its Nasdaq debut, lifting its valuation above USD 2 trillion as investors piled into the world's biggest IPO. The stock opened at USD 150, signalling strong demand and easing concerns over its market debut.

Groceries, fuel, school fees: Is your monthly budget about to get tighter?School fees, petrol, vegetables, utility bills—the list of monthly expenses never seems to end. As inflation rises, many families are asking the same question: is their budget about to get even tighter?

Inflation rises to 3.93% for May as West Asia conflict pushes up fuel and food pricesFood inflation continued to move higher in May. It stood at 4.78%, up from 4.20% in April, showing that prices of food items are rising faster than they were a month earlier.

Markets rally on easing West Asia tensions, oil falling below $88The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Salary mismatch to missed interest: 7 ITR mistakes that can trigger tax noticesFiling your ITR may seem straightforward, but even a minor reporting error can attract the attention of the tax department. Here are seven mistakes you should watch out for.

The world's biggest IPO starts trading today. Is Elon Musk's SpaceX overvalued?SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq after a record $75 billion IPO and a $1.75 trillion valuation. The debut has sharpened questions over valuation, Musk's control, xAI losses and broader market liquidity.

FD rates up to 8.10%: Check these small finance banks offering the highest returnsIn a world of rising uncertainty, guaranteed returns are becoming more valuable. Here's a look at the small finance banks offering FD rates as high as 8.10% this month.

Govt's new petrol, diesel order: Who is affected, and why was it introduced?The Centre has restricted industrial and commercial bulk fuel purchases from retail pumps. The move aims to protect pump supplies for ordinary consumers amid a wide retail-bulk price gap.

Earning Rs 20 lakh? Claim these tax benefits under the new tax regimeThink the new tax regime leaves no room for tax savings? If you earn Rs 20 lakh a year, you may be surprised to learn that several tax-friendly benefits are still available.
Gold demand picks up despite PM's appeal to avoid buyingAt the time of writing at 11.45 am, MCX gold was trading at Rs 1,49,942, up Rs 1,021, while silver stood at Rs 2,42,232, higher by Rs 2,579.

India may let fiscal deficit widen as US-Iran war raises oil costsIndia may allow its fiscal deficit to widen to 4.8% of GDP as the Iran war raises oil-linked costs. The move reflects pressure from higher subsidies, costlier imports and possible spending cuts.

Inflation fears return: Will Indians have to spend more on everyday items?Rising global oil prices and stronger US inflation are reviving concerns over India's household costs. Economists and the RBI say risks are building even without an immediate inflation shock.

Sensex jumped 1,700 points: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Markets surge as crude oil falls below $90 amid hopes of US-Iran breakthroughThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 873.88 points, or 1.18%, to 74,706.43 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 climbed 236.40 points, or 1.02%, to 23,398.00.

World's first trillionaire Elon Musk is now richer than Taiwan after SpaceX IPOElon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX's record IPO lifted his holdings. The milestone pushed his fortune past Taiwan's economy and widened the gap with every other billionaire.

Centre restricts bulk industrial fuel purchases at petrol pumpsThe government has barred industrial, commercial and institutional users from buying petrol and diesel at retail pumps. The move seeks to prevent diversion, curb shortages and protect supplies for ordinary vehicle owners.

US drug agency puts Dabur products on import alert after inspection flags lapsesThe US FDA has placed drugs manufactured at Dabur India's Dadra and Nagar Haveli factory under an import alert after an inspection found data integrity and maintenance lapses.

entertainment.headline: Chiranjeevi admits he's jealous of Ram Charan's Peddi role: Little hard to accept Megastar Chiranjeevi expressed pride and a tinge of jealousy over son Ram Charan's film Peddi, praising both the performance and its inspiring sports drama theme. He revealed that Peddi fulfilled a long-held desire of his own to star in such a film.

Kartik Aaryan marks 2 years of Chandu Champion, hints at a comeback Kartik Aaryan marked two years of Chandu Champion with a heartfelt note reflecting on the film and his transformation. The actor thanked director Kabir Khan and the team while promising that champions will be back.

Kunal Kamra takes a sharp jibe at Pranit More's apology over Rs 370 biryani row Kunal Kamra mocked Pranit More's apology as the Rs 370 biryani row from his show continued. The exchange has kept attention on Himanshu Jangra's remark, the backlash and More's response.

No shortcuts: Huma Qureshi hard launches Rachit Singh with emotional note Actor Huma Qureshi shared photos of Rachit Singh and praised his long journey in a warm Instagram post. His playful reply added fresh buzz to months of relationship and wedding speculation.

Vijay-Rashmika honour students in Telangana, offer scholarships to 180 rankholders Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna visited Telangana's Thummanpet to honour meritorious government school students from Achampet Mandal. The event linked a family homecoming with a new scholarship push for 180 students from the region.

Ikka: Sunny Deol, Akshaye Khanna's courtroom drama set for July release on Netflix Sunny Deol and Akshaye Khanna are set to face off in Siddharth P Malhotra's courtroom drama Ikka, which is set to premiere on Netflix. The film pairs a shared past with a legal battle that tests family, principle and belief.

Sweet Magnolias Season 5 review: Serenity still knows how to soothe the soulRelease Date:Thu Jun 11, 2026 Sweet Magnolias returns to Serenity for a quieter fifth season centred on Maddie, Dana Sue and Helen. The new episodes lean on friendship and emotional maturity even as familiar flaws remain.

Kajol, Juhi Chawla tease Aamir Khan at Lagaan event: He has great taste in women Actors Kajol and Juhi Chawla playfully roasted Aamir Khan at the 25th anniversary of Lagaan over his taste in women. Their banter drew laughter at the venue, leaving Aamir visibly shy.

Surbhi Jyoti, husband Sumit Suri welcome first child, a baby girl Television actor Surbhi Jyoti and her husband Sumit Suri have welcomed their first child, a baby girl. The couple announced their daughter's birth on social media as friends and fans sent their wishes.

Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata vs Main Vaapas Aaunga Day 2: Kangana trails behind Diljit Kangana Ranaut's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata and Diljit Dosanjh's Main Vaapas Aaunga continued their theatrical run on Day 2. The Diljit-led romance drama stayed ahead after both films opened to a subdued box office response.

Why is an Indian in first class? Zakir Khan recalls racist treatment on flight A resurfaced podcast clip of comedian Zakir Khan has reignited conversations about racism faced by Indians abroad. In the viral video, he recalls being questioned while travelling first class and shares how he responded to the incident with humour.

Kriti Sanon shields Rashmika as fans mob them and Shahid Kapoor in Pune mall Kriti Sanon held Rashmika Mandanna close as a fan surge disrupted a Cocktail 2 event in Pune. The chaotic exit drew online praise for Kriti's protective gesture and criticism of crowd control.

Haryana CM declares Kangana's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata tax-free, calls it inspiring Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini announced Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata would be tax-free after attending its Chandigarh screening with Kangana Ranaut.

Govind Namdev alleges Kala Hiran makers misled him about film targeting Salman Khan Govind Namdev has alleged that the makers of Kala Hiran misled him about the film he signed. His remarks deepen the row around the project amid Salman Khan's petition and court intervention.

Sushant Singh Rajput death anniversary: Sister Shweta shares a moving note Sushant Singh Rajput's sister, Shweta Singh Kirti, remembered the late actor ahead of his sixth death anniversary with an emotional Instagram note. She also shared an AI-generated image of him as Lord Ram.

Karan Aujla, Virat Kohli's crossover leaves fans counting down to June 21 Virat Kohli and Karan Aujla are set to come together for a One8 event in Delhi on June 21. The announcement has already generated massive buzz online as fans are eager to see the cricket icon and Punjabi hitmaker share the spotlight.

Shaved head and leather jacket: Salman Khan grabs attention at Aamir Khan's event Salman Khan attended the celebrations marking 25 years of Aamir Khan Productions on Saturday. The actor's latest look drew attention as he posed with Aamir Khan and Kareena Kapoor at the star-studded event.

Kangana Ranaut says she has nothing against Diljit Dosanjh: We've never met Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut said her clash with actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh was only over differing views and not personal. Her remarks revisit their 2020 farmers' protests row as their respective new films open on the same day.

Himanshu Jangra apologises again, says viral biryani story wasn't totally true Himanshu Jangra has apologised again amid massive controversy over the Rs 370 biryani remark at comic Pranit More's show. In a conversation with his former boss, he spoke about being trolled, sacked, his family's reaction and why he now regrets attending the event.

general news.headline: Innovation in India's DNA, no longer just consumer but contributor: PM in France Live UpdatesAt Bharat Innovates in Nice, Narendra Modi said India is emerging as a global innovation solutions hub. He said the country is linking innovation with inclusion and sustainability while backing youth and grassroots ecosystems.

14 Indians evacuated after vessel suffers engine failure off Oman coastAll 14 Indian crew members on the merchant vessel Virat 1 were evacuated after an engine failure off the Oman coast. Officials said the emergency was a technical breakdown, not linked to recent attacks on ships in the Gulf of Oman.

Delhi's Tughlakabad fire that killed 3 wasn't accident. A minor executed the planThree people – Pankaj, Sushila Devi, and Sonia Kumari – died in the June 12 fire. Two other members of the family reportedly suffered severe injuries and remain under medical care.

How Pakistan Punjabised POKPakistan has described the illegally occupied territory of India as "Azad" Jammu and Kashmir. But the region's politics, bureaucracy, demographics and identity have all been governed and influenced by its Punjabi military establishment in Islamabad and Rawalpindi, not the people in Muzaffarabad. This is how Shia-Sunni tensions and Punjabi settlers are robbing POK's Kashmiri identity.

Woman, 21, falls 130 feet to death after bungee jump crew forgets to attach ropeMaria Eduarda Rodrigues de Freitas died after falling from a bridge during a bungee jump in Sao Paulo when the safety cord was allegedly not attached. The incident prompted the arrest of six people and intensified scrutiny of safety standards at the site.

Live: PM Modi visits Villa Kerylos with President Macron in France's NicePM Modi France and Slovakia Visit Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has begun his Europe tour with his arrival in Nice, France, where he will hold talks with French President Emmanuel Macron and jointly inaugurate Bharat Innovates. The visit will also take him to Evian and Paris before he travels to Slovakia. A key highlight of the tour will be the G7 Summit in Evian, where Modi is scheduled to meet US President Donald Trump on June 17. Trade negotiations, innovation partnerships, India-France ties and multilateral cooperation are expected to dominate discussions during the visit. Stay with IndiaToday.in for live updates-

Kakoli Dastidar's son sues Mamata, seeks apology over ticket row remarksBaidyanath Ghosh Dastidar has said that he will initiate defamation proceedings against top TMC leaders if they failed to respond appropriately to the legal notice served by him.

From divorce papers to tears of reunion: Delhi couple ends 5-year court battleA divorce hearing in a Delhi courtroom turned into a reunion when Shikha tore up the papers and embraced Saurabh. The moment followed Saurabh's help during her father's medical crisis and abruptly ended their five-year legal fight.

Battle of mismatch? India vs Pakistan promises more hype, less competitionWomen's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur's India and Fatima Sana's Pakistan are set to face off in a crucial Group A clash on Sunday. But is the contest at Edgbaston more about hype than genuine competition?

150 billion litres water a year: India wants to be AI superpower, but it comes with a billIndia's data centres are estimated to have used around 150 billion litres of water annually in 2024-25, a figure projected to rise to about 358 billion litres by 2030, as cited by CEEW.

Ram Temple donation row: SIT formed, employee detained; Vinay Katiyar seeks actionThe Uttar Pradesh government has formed a three-member SIT to investigate alleged irregularities in Ram temple donations. The move comes amid a widening political row and calls for transparent action against those found guilty.

How Vastushastra helped Iranis build Mumbai's cafe cultureWhat if we told you one of Mumbai's most enduring food cultures was born because Vastushastra once deemed their locations inauspicious? The story of the Irani cafes is not just about chai and brun-maska and old Bombay's charm, but about Irani migrants who turned Bombay's unwanted corner plots into some of its most unforgettable cultural spaces.

Fashion designer Marc Jacobs walks into his own store, staff has no clue who he isMarc Jacobs filmed himself shopping at a Marc Jacobs airport boutique while the salesperson appeared not to recognise him.

US now treats advanced AI as nuclear bomb, hence ban on Anthropic Fable & MythosThe US cutting off the access to Anthropic Mythos and Fable 5 has the potential to reshape the geo-politics, as well as how countries and organisations look at AI from the US-based companies. Or, as Sridhar Vembu from Zoho says, "globalisation is dead."

Indian-origin footballer debuts as Australia cause mega-upset in FIFA World CupIndian-origin winger Nishan Velupillay made his FIFA World Cup debut as Australia beat Turkiye 2-0 in their Group D opener. The result gave the Socceroos an early edge in the qualification race and marked another step in Velupillay's rapid rise.

Why is an Indian in first class? Zakir Khan recalls racist treatment on flightA resurfaced podcast clip of comedian Zakir Khan has reignited conversations about racism faced by Indians abroad. In the viral video, he recalls being questioned while travelling first class and shares how he responded to the incident with humour.

Hampi's singing pillars: The secret behind 500-year-old acoustic masonryThe musical pillars of Hampi's Vitthala temple ring like bells when struck, and scientists at a nuclear research centre have finally explained how solid granite makes music.

Why your salary is losing the race against home pricesIndia's housing dream is facing its biggest affordability test as rising prices force buyers to choose between bigger debts, longer commutes and delayed dreams.

26-year-old Indian man stabbed to death in LondonA brutal knife attack on a west London street left a 26-year-old Indian-origin man dead despite desperate efforts to save him. As police piece together the final moments, chilling questions remain unanswered.

This could have been an email: Why pointless meetings are a corporate life ritualHandcrafted ByOur Editors Open Reddit and search "pointless meetings". You'll find hundreds of posts spanning years, industries and countries, all describing the same thing: meetings with little to discuss and nowhere to go. Employees hate them. Managers schedule them. Somehow, the cycle never ends.

Are water bars the next big Indian luxury wedding trend?Handcrafted ByOur Editors Who knew water could do more than just hydrate. Today, it has the potential to become the next most talked about thing at luxury weddings.

El Nino has arrived: What is Indian Ocean Dipole that can save monsoon?How or if the Indian Ocean Dipole develops remains to be seen. However, if one looks at history, the climate phenomenon does offer hope.

Boyfriend duties call, but...: Trudeau on USA World Cup appearance with Katy PerryJustin Trudeau attended the USA-Paraguay World Cup opener with Katy Perry in Los Angeles. His 'boyfriend duties' explanation sparked online debate as Canada began its own campaign.

Couple claims DNA test showed IVF twins not biologically theirs, demands answersIn an exclusive interview with India Today, the couple claimed that they went through the IVF treatment at a reputed hospital. However, when the babies were born, they bore no resemblance to them.

sports.headline: No Spanish at World Cup? Hakimi, Vini Jr silenced by bizarre FIFA media rules Achraf Hakimi and Vinicius Junior were discouraged from speaking Spanish during World Cup media sessions ahead of Brazil's opener against Morocco. Both players were left stunned by the media rules from FIFA.

India vs Pakistan Live Score: Birmingham set for blockbuster clash India (IND-W) vs Pakistan (PAK-W) Live Score, Women's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur's India and Fatima Sana's Pakistan are all set to face each other in the blockbuster Group A clash on Sunday at Edgbaston in Birmingham.

Mohsin Naqvi set to bring radical changes to PCB amid Pakistan cricket's struggles PCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi plans to expand the cricket advisory panel by adding more former Pakistan players, believing greater cricketing expertise will lead to better decisions and improved results.

Neeraj Chopra named in India's CWG athletics squad despite injury woes The Athletics Federation of India named India's 32-member Commonwealth Games squad on Sunday, June 14. Headlined by sprinters Gurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur, the squad also includes Neeraj Chopra, who is currently recovering from his back injury.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Turkiye defeated by Australia's height in Vancouver FIFA World Cup 2026: Australia beat Turkiye 2-0 in Vancouver with a ruthless counterattacking display and a defensive line Turkiye could not breach. The result exposed the limits of star power against structure and delivered the first major upset of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

World Cup: Germany coach Nagelsmann confirms Neuer's return for Curacao game Germany coach Julian Nagelsmann confirmed on Saturday that veteran goalkeeper Manuel Neuer will start against Curacao on Sunday, marking his return to international football after nearly two years and his fifth FIFA World Cup appearance.

From refugee camp to FIFA World Cup glory: Australia's Irankunda rewrites history FIFA World Cup 2026: Australia beat Türkiye 2-0 in Vancouver as Nestory Irankunda scored from a rapid counterattack. The goal made him Australia's youngest World Cup scorer and sharpened the scale of his rise from Kigoma refugee camp to the elite game.

Indian-origin footballer debuts as Australia cause mega-upset in FIFA World Cup Indian-origin winger Nishan Velupillay made his FIFA World Cup debut as Australia beat Turkiye 2-0 in their Group D opener. The result gave the Socceroos an early edge in the qualification race and marked another step in Velupillay's rapid rise.

Australia stun Turkiye 2-0 in Vancouver to deliver first World Cup upset FIFA World Cup 2026: Australia beat Turkiye 2-0 in Vancouver through sharp counter-attacking and stubborn defending. The result jolts Group D, pushes the Socceroos towards the top and leaves Turkiye needing a response.

England's stolen football gear worth Rs 17 lakh recovered 24 hours after theft England football team's stolen gear, reportedly worth around Rs 17 lakh, was recovered by Kansas City police within 24 hours of the theft. Two men from Texas have been charged with felony counts by authorities.

The first FIFA World Cup memory that stayed with our newsroomNot every World Cup memory is shaped by what happened on the pitch. For members of India Today's News Desk, the tournament is also about the people, moments and emotions that turned football's biggest stage into something deeply personal. Comment down below about your first World Cup memory.

Robbery? Scotland vs Haiti marred by bad referee as fans outrage over social mediaFIFA World Cup 2026: Scotland opened their World Cup return with a 1-0 win over Haiti after John McGinn's deflected goal. The result brought three points, but disputed VAR calls and Haiti's bold display shaped the wider argument.

NBA: New York Knicks beat Spurs to claim first NBA title in 53 yearsThe New York Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 on Saturday to secure their first NBA championship since 1973. Led by a game-high 45 points from Jalen Brunson, the Knicks wrapped up the best-of-seven NBA Finals 4-1.

Scotland edge Haiti to secure first World Cup win in 36 yearsScotland marked their return to the FIFA World Cup with a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Haiti on Saturday, securing their first win at the tournament since 1990 and moving to the top of Group C.

Carlo Ancelotti slams 'nervous' Brazil after Morocco drawBrazil coach Carlo Ancelotti admitted his side must improve after being held to a 1-1 draw by Morocco national football team in their opening Group C match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Saturday, describing Brazil's first-half display as "nervous" and unbalanced.

FIFA clarifies VAR controversy in Switzerland vs Qatar after fan outrageFIFA has clarified the controversial VAR decision that dominated discussions during Switzerland's 1-1 draw against Qatar at the FIFA World Cup 2026, revealing that a brief technical issue prevented the offside animation graphic from being shown to viewers.

Australia vs Turkey Highlights: Australia stuns Turkiye, Scotland beat HaitiAustralia (AUS) vs Turkey (TUR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Australia stuns Turkiye in their Group D opener, after Nestory Irankunda and Metcalfe produced a moment of counterattacking brilliance to hand the Socceroos a 2-0 win over Turkiye. Earlier in the day, Scotland moved to the top of Group C with a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Haiti. Despite Haiti dominating possession and pressing high for long periods, Scotland struck against the run of play through a deflected John McGinn effort and held on to secure all three points.

Battle of mismatch? India vs Pakistan promises more hype, less competitionWomen's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur's India and Fatima Sana's Pakistan are set to face off in a crucial Group A clash on Sunday. But is the contest at Edgbaston more about hype than genuine competition?

Brazil's quest for sixth star starts with a stumble as Morocco earn 1-1 drawFIFA World Cup 2026: Carlo Ancelotti's World Cup debut as Brazil manager ended without the trademark Joga Bonito as the five-time champions were held to a 1-1 draw by Morocco in New Jersey. Ismael Saibari fired the Atlas Lions ahead before Vinicius Junior produced a moment of brilliance to rescue a point in a fiercely contested Group C opener.

Vinicius Jr's World Cup stunner sends Travis Scott wild in Brazil vs Morocco clashVinicius Junior produced a stunning equaliser against Morocco in Brazil's FIFA World Cup 2026 opener, with the spectacular strike leaving fans in awe and American rapper Travis Scott celebrating wildly in the stands.

Even my building watchman says, lose to anyone but not Pakistan: JemimahJemimah Rodrigues said India cannot ignore the pressure of facing Pakistan at the Women's T20 World Cup. Her remarks underlined the rivalry's weight as India prepare for high-pressure moments through scenario-based training.

Brazil vs Morocco spoilt by streaming glitches for World Cup fans in IndiaFIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil's World Cup opener against Morocco should have been a marquee occasion for Indian football fans. Instead, many viewers once again reported crashes, buffering and login issues, reviving frustrations that first surfaced during the tournament opener.

Was Rohit Sharma angry? Shubman Gill addresses run-out drama in DharamshalaShubman Gill said Rohit Sharma was fine after their run-out mix-up against Afghanistan. The repeat of Mohali's chaos ended without an outburst, even as Gill's 84 and slips catch drove India's win.

Qatar steal historic first World Cup point as Switzerland pay for missed chancesQatar secured the first World Cup point in their history as captain Boualem Khoukhi's stoppage-time header earned a dramatic 1-1 draw against Switzerland, who paid for a host of missed chances.

West Indies' Campbelle survives and sinks defending champions New ZealandShemaine Campbelle's unbeaten 88 helped West Indies chase down 163 in a last-over thriller, handing defending champions New Zealand an early setback after a costly display of dropped catches and missed chances.

Nitish Reddy pauses press conference to take mother's call after India winNitish Kumar Reddy paused his post-match press conference in Dharamsala to take a call from his mother after India's win over Afghanistan. The brief exchange became a talking point online, with fans praising the all-rounder for staying grounded.

Gurnoor steams, Gill flows as India sweep Afghanistan in rain-hit Dharamsala ODIIndia maintained their dominance over Afghanistan in the ongoing bilateral series, thrashing them by seven wickets in the first ODI of the three-match contest. Gujarat Titans stars Gurnoor Brar and Shubman Gill led India's all-round performance against the visitors.

No handshake with Pak? Harmanpreet responds ahead of Women's T20 World Cup clashIndia captain Harmanpreet Kaur avoided the handshake debate ahead of Sunday's Women's T20 World Cup clash against Pakistan, refusing to comment on whether the teams would exchange greetings before the match. Instead, she kept the focus on the cricket as the high-profile rivalry returned to the spotlight.

FIFA World Cup Highlights: Ancelotti's men disappoint in 1-1 drawBrazil (BRA) vs Morocco (MAR), FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: Brazil's World Cup campaign begins with frustration as Morocco earn a deserved draw. Carlo Ancelotti's side struggled for cohesion, while the African champions once again impressed.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
